entries.definition:
كتاب الحاء

حدأ

(حدث) الحاء والدال والثاء أصلٌ واحد، وهو كونُ الشيء لم يكُنْ. يقال حدثَ أمرٌ بَعْد أن لم يكُن. والرجُل الحَدَثُ: الطريُّ السّن. والحديثُ مِنْ هذا؛ لأنّه كلامٌ يحْدُثُ منه الشيءُ بعدَ الشيء. ورجلٌ حدثٌ : حَسَن الحديث. ورجل حِدْثُ نساءٍ، إذا كانَ يتحدَّث إليهنّ. ويقال هذه حِدِّيثى حَسَنَة، كخِطِّيبَى، يراد به الحديثُ.

h–d–th new, novel, youth; to originate, to create; to happen,
incident, occurrence; to tell, to narrate, to speak to, to disclose. Of
this root, five forms occur 36 times in the Qur’an: تُحَدِّثُ tuḥaddith
three times; يُحْدِث yuḥdith three times; مُحْدَث muḥdath twice; حَدِيث
ḥadīth 23 times and أَحَادِيث ʾaḥādīth five times.

تُحَدِّثُ tuḥaddith [imperf. of v. II حَدَّثَ ḥaddatha] 1 to divulge, to
disclose, to tell (something) (99:4) تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبَارَهَا on that Day, it
will tell its story 2 [with prep. بـ] to acquaint someone with, to
tell someone something, to inform someone of (2:76) أَتُحَدِّثُونَهُم بِمَا
فَتَحَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ لِيُحَآجُّوكُم بِهِۦ عِندَ رَبِّكُمْ
how can you disclose to them what
God has revealed to you, so that they may be able to use it to
argue against you before your Lord? 3 to acknowledge, to show
gratitude (93:11) وَأَمَّا بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ فَحَدِّثْ and as for the blessings of
your Lord, so [forever] acknowledge [them].

194
entries.definition_english:
h–d–th new, novel, youth; to originate, to create; to happen,
incident, occurrence; to tell, to narrate, to speak to, to disclose. Of
this root, five forms occur 36 times in the Qur’an: تُحَدِّثُ tuḥaddith
three times; يُحْدِث yuḥdith three times; مُحْدَث muḥdath twice; حَدِيث
ḥadīth 23 times and أَحَادِيث ʾaḥādīth five times.

تُحَدِّثُ tuḥaddith [imperf. of v. II حَدَّثَ ḥaddatha] 1 to divulge, to
disclose, to tell (something) (99:4) تُحَدِّثُ أَخْبَارَهَا on that Day, it
will tell its story 2 [with prep. بـ] to acquaint someone with, to
tell someone something, to inform someone of (2:76) أَتُحَدِّثُونَهُم بِمَا
فَتَحَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ لِيُحَآجُّوكُم بِهِۦ عِندَ رَبِّكُمْ
how can you disclose to them what
God has revealed to you, so that they may be able to use it to
argue against you before your Lord? 3 to acknowledge, to show
gratitude (93:11) وَأَمَّا بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ فَحَدِّثْ and as for the blessings of
your Lord, so [forever] acknowledge [them].

194

entries.definition:
يُحْدِث yuḥdith [imperf. of v. IV أَحْدَثَ ʾaḥdatha] 1 to cause
something to come about (65:1) لَا تَدْرِي لَعَلَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ أَمْرًا you
can never know, for God may very well bring about a [new]
situation 2 to initiate (18:70) فَإِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتَنِي فَلَا تَسْـَٔلْنِي عَن شَيْءٍ حَتَّىٰٓ أُحْدِثَ
لَكَ مِنْهُ ذِكْرًا if you follow me, then, do not ask me about anything till I
[myself] start to make a mention of it to you.

مُحْدَث muḥdath [pass. part.] something newly created, fresh
(21:2) مَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن ذِكْرٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِم مُّحْدَثٍ إِلَّا ٱسْتَمَعُوهُ وَهُمْ يَلْعَبُونَ
no newly
revealed reminder comes to them from their Lord, but they listen
to it as they play.

حَدِيث ḥadīth [n., pl. أَحَادِيث ʾaḥādīth] 1 discourse (52:34) فَلْيَأْتُوا۟
بِحَدِيثٍ مِّثْلِهِۦٓ إِن كَانُوا۟ صَٰدِقِينَ let them produce one discourse like it if
they are truthful 2 conversation, chat (33:53) فَإِذَا طَعِمْتُمْ فَٱنتَشِرُوا۟ وَلَا
مُسْتَـْٔنِسِينَ لِحَدِيثٍ then, when you have eaten, disperse, and do not stay
on desiring a chat 3 statement, tiding, news (53:59) أَفَمِنْ هَٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ
تَعْجَبُونَ do you [people] marvel at this tiding? 4 topic, subject of
conversation (6:68) وَإِذَا رَأَيْتَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخُوضُونَ فِيٓ ءَايَٰتِنَا فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ حَتَّىٰ
يَخُوضُوا۟ فِي حَدِيثٍ غَيْرِهِۦ when you come across people who speak ill of
our revelations, turn away from them until they move on to
another topic 5 piece of information (66:3) وَإِذْ أَسَرَّ ٱلنَّبِيُّ إِلَىٰ بَعْضِ
أَزْوَٰجِهِۦ حَدِيثًا and when the Prophet told something in confidence to
one of his wives 6 story, account, tale (51:24) هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ضَيْفِ
إِبْرَٰهِيمَ ٱلْمُكْرَمِينَ has the story of the honoured guests of Abraham
reached you? 7 lesson, example, cautionary tale (23:44) كُلَّ مَا جَآءَ
أُمَّةً رَّسُولُهَا كَذَّبُوهُ فَأَتْبَعْنَا بَعْضَهُم بَعْضًا وَجَعَلْنَٰهُمْ أَحَادِيثَ whenever a
messenger came to a community, they called him a liar, so We
destroyed them one after the other and made them into cautionary
tales 8 events, happenings (in an interpretation of 12:6) وَكَذَٰلِكَ
يَجْتَبِيكَ رَبُّكَ وَيُعَلِّمُكَ مِن تَأْوِيلِ ٱلْأَحَادِيثِ وَيُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُۥ عَلَيْكَ
thus, your Lord will choose
you, teach you to interpret events and perfect His blessing on you
9 dreams (in an interpretation of 12:6) وَكَذَٰلِكَ يَجْتَبِيكَ رَبُّكَ وَيُعَلِّمُكَ
مِن تَأْوِيلِ ٱلْأَحَادِيثِ وَيُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُۥ عَلَيْكَ
thus, your Lord will choose you, teach
you to interpret dreams and perfect His blessing on you.

thus, your Lord will choose

9 dreams (in an interpretation of 12:6) وَكَذَٰلِكَ يَجْتَبِيكَ رَبُّكَ وَيُعَلِّمُكَ

thus, your Lord will choose you, teach
you to interpret dreams and perfect His blessing on you.
